verses.1: Wine is a mocker, intoxicating drink a brawler, And whoever is intoxicated by it is not wise.
verses.2: The terror of a king is like the roaring of a lion; One who provokes him to anger forfeits his own life.
verses.3: Avoiding strife is an honor for a person, But any fool will quarrel.
verses.4: The lazy one does not plow after the autumn, So he begs during the harvest and has nothing.
verses.5: A plan in the heart of a person is like deep water, But a person of understanding draws it out.
verses.6: Many a person proclaims his own loyalty, But who can find a trustworthy person?
verses.7: A righteous person who walks in his integrity— How blessed are his sons after him.
verses.8: A king who sits on the throne of justice Disperses all evil with his eyes.
verses.9: Who can say, “I have cleansed my heart, I am pure from my sin”?
verses.10: Differing weights and differing measures, Both of them are abominable to the LORD.
verses.11: It is by his deeds that a boy distinguishes himself, If his conduct is pure and right.
verses.12: The hearing ear and the seeing eye, The LORD has made both of them.
verses.13: Do not love sleep, or you will become poor; Open your eyes, and you will be satisfied with food.
verses.14: “Bad, bad,” says the buyer, But when he goes his way, then he boasts.
verses.15: There is gold, and an abundance of jewels; But lips of knowledge are a more precious thing.
verses.16: Take his garment when he becomes guarantor for a stranger; And for foreigners, seize a pledge from him.
verses.17: Bread obtained by a lie is sweet to a person, But afterward his mouth will be filled with gravel.
verses.18: Prepare plans by consultation, And make war by wise guidance.
verses.19: One who goes about as a slanderer reveals secrets; Therefore do not associate with a gossip.
verses.20: He who curses his father or his mother, His lamp will go out in time of darkness.
verses.21: An inheritance gained in a hurry at the beginning Will not be blessed in the end.
verses.22: Do not say, “I will repay evil”; Wait for the LORD, and He will save you.
verses.23: Differing weights are an abomination to the LORD, And a false scale is not good.
verses.24: A man’s steps are ordained by the LORD; How then can a person understand his way?
verses.25: It is a trap for a person to say carelessly, “It is holy!” And after the vows to make inquiry.
verses.26: A wise king scatters the wicked, And drives a threshing wheel over them.
verses.27: The spirit of a person is the lamp of the LORD, Searching all the innermost parts of his being.
verses.28: Loyalty and truth watch over the king, And he upholds his throne by loyalty.
verses.29: The glory of young men is their strength, And the honor of old men is their gray hair.
verses.30: Bruising wounds clean away evil, And blows cleanse the innermost parts.
